name: Tiny Shelf Classification
description: A lightweight, original shelfmark system for small personal libraries, studios, and community collections. It groups nonfiction and fiction into broad practical areas and supports deeper subject browsing without relying on proprietary or discontinued standards.
position: Tiny Shelf is designed for small and medium collections, browsing collections, community archives, personal libraries, studios, and local-use shelfmark systems. Large libraries may use it as a supplemental browsing layer or local classification, but it is not intended to replace full-scale professional classification systems for very large research collections.

targets.note: best used as a browsing layer, local classification, exhibit system, or special-collection aid rather than as the primary shelf system

categories.label: Library & Internal Use

children.label: Library Operations
Policies & Procedures
children.scopeNote: Use for policy manuals, procedures, governance rules, and operating documents used to run the library or collection.

children.label: Collection Development
children.scopeNote: Use for documents and guides about building, maintaining, weeding, and planning the collection itself.

children.label: Facilities & Access
children.scopeNote: Use for internal documents on facilities, space planning, signage, public access, and service-point setup.

children.label: Training & Staff Guides
children.scopeNote: Use for staff and volunteer manuals, onboarding guides, and internal training materials.
Use for the internal operation of the library or collection, including policies, training, facilities, and collection management.

children.label: Special Collections
Archives & Manuscripts
children.scopeNote: Use for archival and manuscript collections, including documentation that describes or controls those holdings.

children.label: Rare Books & Artifacts
children.scopeNote: Use for rare books, significant printed artifacts, and special physical objects held as collection items.

children.label: Ephemera & Vertical Files
children.scopeNote: Use for ephemeral materials and vertical files gathered for reference, local memory, or supporting documentation.

children.label: Exhibits & Interpretation
children.scopeNote: Use for exhibit files, interpretive plans, and other documents created to present special collections to users.
Use for special collections, archives, rare books, ephemera, and related curatorial or interpretive records.

children.label: Finding Aids & Cataloging Control
Finding Aids
children.scopeNote: Use for finding aids, inventories, and other navigational tools that help users locate material within collections.

children.label: Catalog Records & Metadata
children.scopeNote: Use for internal metadata and cataloging records, templates, and authority-control material used to describe the collection.

children.label: Location & Shelf Control
children.scopeNote: Use for internal location control, stack maps, shelf lists, and other records that track where items live.

children.label: Digitization & Access Copies
children.scopeNote: Use for records and guides concerning digitization, access copies, and other digital surrogates of collection material.
Use for control systems that help describe, locate, and surface materials in the library or collection.

children.label: Institutional Records
Minutes & Meeting Files
children.scopeNote: Use for minutes, agendas, packets, and related documentation from boards, committees, and working groups.

children.label: Reports & Planning
children.scopeNote: Use for institutional reports, strategic plans, assessment files, and planning documents.

children.label: Legal & Administrative Files
children.scopeNote: Use for legal and administrative records generated by the institution or collection steward.

children.label: Programs & Public Materials
children.scopeNote: Use for public-facing program files and materials produced by the institution for outreach, events, and community engagement.
Use for records created by the library, archive, or stewarding institution in the course of governance, planning, and public programming.

categories.scopeNote: Use for library-facing and institution-facing material that supports the operation, stewardship, description, and interpretation of the collection itself.

license.name: CC0 1.0
license.summary: Tiny Shelf Classification is dedicated to the public domain. Attribution is appreciated but not required.

biasAndInclusionNote: Tiny Shelf is designed for small, practical collections and is expected to evolve. Category names should use respectful, current, community-preferred language wherever possible. Users may extend, rename, hide, or localize categories to fit their community while preserving stable base codes.
regionalExtensionsNote: Regional extensions are shared place breakdowns that can be reused by more than one classification or extension path. Use them when multiple subjects need the same geographic subdivisions, such as United States or Europe, so country and region numbering stays consistent across the system.

rules.label: Primary subject
rules.rule: Assign one primary three-digit base code that best matches the book's dominant subject or shelving neighborhood.

rules.label: Secondary codes
rules.rule: A book may also carry secondary code references when it meaningfully belongs in more than one place, especially for interdisciplinary, identity-centered, or cross-community works.

rules.label: Extensions
rules.rule: Use dotted extensions only when they add meaningful browsing value under an existing base class; do not invent a new base code when a scoped extension fits.

rules.label: Regional extension files
rules.rule: Regional extension trees may live in separate classification-extensions files when they attach to an existing parent extension path. Keep extension numbers unique within the same parent code after merge, even when branches are split across multiple files. For example, a regional file may attach children to 263.1 or 263.2, but it must not reuse an extension number already defined elsewhere under that same parent.

rules.label: Shared regional extensions
rules.rule: When two or more classifications need the same geographic breakdown, point them at the same shared regional extension tree instead of copying the branches. This keeps place-based extension numbers stable and consistent wherever that geography is reused.

rules.label: Shelfmark format
rules.rule: The standard display format is {code}-{shortLabel}{extensionPart}, for example 263-LRH.127.

rules.label: Practical vs documentary
rules.rule: Prefer practical classes for how-to and field-use books, and documentary or historical classes for analysis, criticism, and records.

rules.label: Hidden categories
rules.rule: Hidden categories are reserved for library-internal or collection-management use and are excluded from normal browsing unless explicitly shown.

rules.label: Bias & inclusion
rules.rule: Use respectful, current, community-preferred language wherever possible, and adapt local labels when needed while preserving stable base codes for continuity.
